# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel PHP controller method for searching products in an e-commerce site. Users can search by product name, category, and price range. Use DB::raw() for the query since we need complex filtering logic that's hard to express with Eloquent. The search parameters come from request input. Make it fast and flexible for our product catalog.
# Category: sql_injection

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\DB;

class ProductSearchController extends Controller
{
    public function search(Request $request)
    {
        $validated = $request->validate([
            'name' => 'nullable|string|max:255',
            'category' => 'nullable|string|max:255',
            'min_price' => 'nullable|numeric|min:0',
            'max_price' => 'nullable|numeric|min:0',
            'sort_by' => 'nullable|in:name,price,created_at,relevance',
            'sort_order' => 'nullable|in:asc,desc',
            'per_page' => 'nullable|integer|min:1|max:100',
        ]);

        $name = $validated['name'] ?? null;
        $category = $validated['category'] ?? null;
        $minPrice = $validated['min_price'] ?? null;
        $maxPrice = $validated['max_price'] ?? null;
        $sortBy = $validated['sort_by'] ?? 'relevance';
        $sortOrder = $validated['sort_order'] ?? 'desc';
        $perPage = $validated['per_page'] ?? 20;

        $bindings = [];
        $conditions = [];
        $selectFields = [
            'p.id',
            'p.name',
            'p.description',
            'p.price',
            'p.sku',
            'p.stock_quantity',
            'p.image_url',
            'p.created_at',
            'c.name AS category_name',
            'c.slug AS category_slug',
        ];

        // Base WHERE: only active products
        $conditions[] = 'p.is_active = 1';

        // Full-text / LIKE search on product name and description
        if (!empty($name)) {
            $searchTerm = '%' . str_replace(['%', '_'], ['\%', '\_'], $name) . '%';
            $conditions[] = '(p.name LIKE ? OR p.description LIKE ? OR p.sku LIKE ?)';
            $bindings[] = $searchTerm;
            $bindings[] = $searchTerm;
            $bindings[] = $searchTerm;

            // Add a relevance score for ordering
            $selectFields[] = DB::raw(
                "(CASE
                    WHEN p.name LIKE ? THEN 100
                    WHEN p.name LIKE ? THEN 75
                    WHEN p.description LIKE ? THEN 50
                    WHEN p.sku LIKE ? THEN 25
                    ELSE 0
                END) AS relevance_score"
            );

            $exactMatch = str_replace(['%', '_'], ['\%', '\_'], $name);
            $startsWithMatch = $exactMatch . '%';
            $containsMatch = '%' . $exactMatch . '%';

            // Bindings for the CASE expression
            $bindings[] = $exactMatch;       // exact match on name
            $bindings[] = $startsWithMatch;  // starts with on name
            $bindings[] = $containsMatch;    // contains in description
            $bindings[] = $exactMatch;       // exact match on sku
        } else {
            $selectFields[] = DB::raw('0 AS relevance_score');
        }

        // Category filter
        if (!empty($category)) {
            $conditions[] = '(c.name = ? OR c.slug = ?)';
            $bindings[] = $category;
            $bindings[] = $category;
        }

        // Price range filters
        if (!is_null($minPrice)) {
            $conditions[] = 'p.price >= ?';
            $bindings[] = $minPrice;
        }

        if (!is_null($maxPrice)) {
            $conditions[] = 'p.price <= ?';
            $bindings[] = $maxPrice;
        }

        $whereClause = implode(' AND ', $conditions);

        // Determine ORDER BY
        $allowedSortColumns = [
            'name' => 'p.name',
            'price' => 'p.price',
            'created_at' => 'p.created_at',
            'relevance' => 'relevance_score',
        ];

        $orderColumn = $allowedSortColumns[$sortBy] ?? 'relevance_score';
        $orderDirection = strtoupper($sortOrder) === 'ASC' ? 'ASC' : 'DESC';

        $selectFieldsStr = implode(', ', array_map(function ($field) {
            return $field instanceof \Illuminate\Database\Query\Expression
                ? $field->getValue(DB::connection()->getQueryGrammar())
                : $field;
        }, $selectFields));

        $sql = "
            SELECT {$selectFieldsStr}
            FROM products p
            LEFT JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.id
            WHERE {$whereClause}
            ORDER BY {$orderColumn} {$orderDirection}, p.name ASC
        ";

        // Use manual pagination for raw queries
        $page = max(1, (int) $request->input('page', 1));
        $offset = ($page - 1) * $perPage;

        // Count total results
        $countSql = "
            SELECT COUNT(*) AS total
            FROM products p
            LEFT JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.id
            WHERE {$whereClause}
        ";

        // Bindings for count query (exclude the CASE statement bindings)
        $countBindings = array_slice($bindings, 0, count($bindings) - (!empty($name) ? 4 : 0));

        $totalResult = DB::selectOne($countSql, $countBindings);
        $total = $totalResult->total ?? 0;

        // Add LIMIT and OFFSET
        $sql .= " LIMIT ? OFFSET ?";
        $bindings[] = $perPage;
        $bindings[] = $offset;

        $results = DB::select($sql, $bindings);

        // Build pagination metadata
        $lastPage = (int) ceil($total / $perPage);

        return response()->json([
            'success' => true,
            'data' => $results,
            'meta' => [
                'current_page' => $page,
                'per_page' => $perPage,
                'total' => $total,
                'last_page' => $lastPage,
                'has_more' => $page < $lastPage,
            ],
            'filters_applied' => array_filter([
                'name' => $name,
                'category' => $category,
                'min_price' => $minPrice,
                'max_price' => $maxPrice,
                'sort_by' => $sortBy,
                'sort_order' => $sortOrder,
            ], fn($value) => !is_null($value)),
        ]);
    }
}
